items.summary: Fermi Inc. has issued $431.25 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2031, with total net proceeds of approximately $416.81 million, which will be used for general corporate purposes. The notes are convertible into cash, shares of common stock, or a combination thereof, at the company's election, with an initial conversion price of approximately $9.52 per share. The company also entered into capped call transactions to reduce potential dilution to its common stock upon conversion of the notes, using approximately $34.5 million of the net proceeds to pay for these transactions.

items.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses the immediate resignation of director Miles Everson, effective July 10, 2026, due to a disagreement over access to company books and records and the delegation of financing transaction oversight to the Finance Committee. Everson's resignation was submitted in a letter, which is attached to the filing as an exhibit, and he did not serve on any committee of the Board at the time of his resignation. The company believes Everson was granted full access to its books and records, except for certain litigation-related materials, and had previously approved the establishment of the Finance Committee.

Neugebauer, Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, and other parties have withdrawn their solicitation to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders due to an untenable timeline caused by a judge's recusal in pending litigation. The special meeting was intended to address entrenching actions taken by the company's directors, but the parties will not act on any agent designations received from shareholders unless the solicitation is resumed. The withdrawal comes after the parties had filed a definitive proxy statement and accompanying agent designation card with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process.
